flashcards.definition: Add complex exponentials instead of trig functions; the resultant's magnitude and phase follow directly.
A complex-valued function; its modulus squared |ψ|² gives probability density.
P(x) = |ψ(x)|²; probability from the modulus of a complex wave function.
Results from adding wave phasors: constructive when in phase, destructive when π out of phase.
e^(iθ) = cosθ + i sinθ; connects exponential and trig representations.
